features.title: Each ticket an AI chat-box
features.desc: Chat with your agent right in the dock, with the active ticket’s context attached.

image.alt: Opening a ticket’s chat with its context already attached

features.details: Every ticket carries its own chat. Open it and the agent already has the ticket’s full context — description, history, branch and attachments — so you never re-explain. Minimise it to the dock and it keeps running in the background while you work elsewhere.

features.title: Multi-agent orchestration
features.desc: Delegate to specialist agents or fan work out scatter-gather under a supervisor.
features.details: Hand a ticket to a specialist persona, or fan the work out scatter-gather under a supervisor that reviews and synthesises the results.  then watch every session run live on the board.

features.title: Design your own Workflows
features.desc: Make workflows that fit your needs, and implement your own agent personas and skills.
features.details: Use various orchestration types, and add Agent personas that are tailor made to your product or workflows.

features.title: Isolated Agent runs
features.desc: Run each ticket in its own git branched worktree - parallel work has never been easier.

image.alt: Running a ticket in its own isolated worktree branch

features.details: Each ticket runs in its own git worktree on its own branch, so two agents can work different tickets at the same time with zero file collisions. Commit before Ready and Event Horizon opens the PR for you.

features.title: Inline PRs
features.desc: Finish a ticket and Event Horizon opens a PR for you; review it, then lets you merge straight to Done.
features.details: Move a ticket to Ready and its branch is pushed and a pull request opened automatically. Review the diff on GitHub, then ‘finish’ squash-merges it and advances the ticket to Done — no terminal, no manual git dance.

features.title: Docs & shared workspace
features.desc: Built-in markdown docs, plus group docs shared across every repo in a multi-repo group.
features.details: Project docs live as plain markdown in your repo and render right in the portal. Link several repos into a group and a shared knowledge base fans out to every member, so cross-repo work has one source of truth.

features.title: Multi-repo groups
features.desc: Link several repos into one product and map features across them.
features.details: Bind several repositories into a single product. Map how a feature crosses repo boundaries, share docs and context across all of them, and orchestrate work spanning the whole group from one board.

features.title: Version Control, Release Notes
features.desc: Release new versions, create changelogs and more straight from the board.
features.details: Cut releases straight from the board: gather what shipped, auto-generate a changelog from your Done tickets, and tag a version — your release history without ever leaving Event Horizon.

features.title: Board Orchestrator
features.desc: Talk to the orchestrator about your project and board, coordinating the nitty gritty from a centralized location.
features.details: Talk to the orchestrator about your whole project — it triages the board, breaks big asks into tickets, dispatches the right agents, and keeps the nitty-gritty coordinated from one place.

features.title: Yours, in markdown
features.desc: Every ticket is a plain markdown file in your repo — version-controlled, diffable, and yours.
features.details: Event Horizon is local-first: tickets are markdown files committed to your repo (or a dedicated data branch), so they version alongside your code, work offline, and never get trapped in someone else’s database. Switch machines and your whole board comes with you.
